title: nauclea zeylanica
intro: Nauclea zeylanica, also known as quinine tree or nauclea, is a deciduous tree native to Southeast Asia that produces medicinal and edible properties. The bark and leaves of the plant have been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. Nauclea zeylanica has been used to treat various health conditions, including malaria, fever, and digestive issues, as well as to relieve pain and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Nauclea zeylanica include tea infusions, extracts, and capsules, which can be found in some natural health stores and online platforms, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
